close_reason: Accepted. source() returning Box<dyn Error> is the standard error chain pattern. Callers should use Display, not downcast. Wrapping SDK errors in a local enum would lose error chain context without improving the API.
description: SecretError::Backend { source: Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync> } and SecretError::Unavailable { source: Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync> } in secretx-core/src/lib.rs use type-erased error boxes. In practice, multiple backends stuff SDK-specific structured errors directly into these boxes: AwsSmBackend puts SdkError<GetSecretValueError> into source, AzureKvBackend puts azure_core::Error, etc. Callers who downcast source to inspect the root cause are implicitly depending on a specific backend's SDK error type, creating a hidden coupling that is not visible in the public API. What goes wrong: a caller downcasting source to aws_sdk_secretsmanager::operation::get_secret_value::GetSecretValueError will get None if the backend is later changed to use a different SDK version or error wrapping strategy. The public API gives no indication that downcast targets are stable. Fix: document explicitly that the concrete type behind source is not a stable contract and must not be downcast in portable code, or introduce per-backend error variants if inspectability is required.
title: SecretError::Backend/Unavailable.source leaks SDK error types
